title: Permission denied when starting .container file with quadlet on WSL2 on Windows 11
body:
### Issue Description

I have a .container file that I would like to start using systemctl --user start filename, but it fails with a permission denied error.



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install Ubuntu/Debian/Mint in WSL2 
2. Install Podman 
3. Create a container file 
4. run systemctl --user daemon-reload
5. run systemctl --user start filename


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you receivedError: mkdir /sys/fs/cgroup/pids/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/runtime: permission denied

### Describe the results you expected

To start the container successfully

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: Can you try with the `systemd` cgroup manager, instead of `cgroupfs`?
Sorry I don't know what you mean with that exactly?
In `containers.conf` on the Ubuntu WSL machine, please set `cgroup_manager="systemd"` - you should be able to find it at `/etc/containers/containers.conf` or `/usr/share/containers/containers.conf`

Check. 

Changed the setting as suggested to systemd, however I think that is default, rebooted, same error
It seems WSL2 is using cgroups v1 where rootless Quadlet won't work. Can you retry running as root?
Running as root it works. No errors
I guess you need to convert your ubuntu/wsl to use cgroups V2 (Something I would advise) or run in rootful mode.
